fields.name: SU
fields.description: Second units in BCD format

fields.name: ST
fields.description: Second tens in BCD format

fields.name: MNU
fields.description: Minute units in BCD format

fields.name: MNT
fields.description: Minute tens in BCD format

fields.name: HU
fields.description: Hour units in BCD format

fields.name: HT
fields.description: Hour tens in BCD format

fields.name: PM
fields.description: AM/PM notation

fields.name: DU
fields.description: Date units in BCD format

fields.name: DT
fields.description: Date tens in BCD format

fields.name: MU
fields.description: Month units in BCD format

fields.name: MT
fields.description: Month tens in BCD format

fields.name: WDU
fields.description: Week day units ...

fields.name: YU
fields.description: Year units in BCD format

fields.name: YT
fields.description: Year tens in BCD format

fields.name: SS
fields.description: Synchronous binary counter SS[31:16]: Synchronous binary counter MSB values When Binary or Mixed mode is selected (BIN = 01 or 10 or 11): SS[31:16] are the 16 MSB of the SS[31:0] free-running down-counter. When BCD mode is selected (BIN=00): SS[31:16] are forced by hardware to 0x0000. SS[15:0]: Sub second value/Synchronous binary counter LSB values When Binary mode is selected (BIN = 01 or 10 or 11): SS[15:0] are the 16 LSB of the SS[31:0] free-running down-counter. When BCD mode is selected (BIN=00): SS[15:0] is the value in the synchronous prescaler counter. The fraction of a second is given by the formula below: Second fraction = (PREDIV_S SS) / (PREDIV_S + 1) SS can be larger than PREDIV_S only after a shift operation. In that case, the correct time/date is one second less than as indicated by RTC_TR/RTC_DR.

registers.name: ICSR
registers.description: RTC initialization control and status register

fields.name: WUTWF
fields.description: Wakeup timer write flag This bit is set by hardware when WUT value can be changed, after the WUTE bit has been set to 0 in RTC_CR. It is cleared by hardware in initialization mode.

enum_values.name: UpdateNotAllowed
enum_values.description: Wakeup timer configuration update not allowed

fields.name: SHPF
fields.description: Shift operation pending This flag is set by hardware as soon as a shift operation is initiated by a write to the RTC_SHIFTR register. It is cleared by hardware when the corresponding shift operation has been executed. Writing to the SHPF bit has no effect.

fields.name: INITS
fields.description: Initialization status flag This bit is set by hardware when the calendar year field is different from 0 (Backup domain reset state).

fields.name: RSF
fields.description: Registers synchronization flag This bit is set by hardware each time the calendar registers are copied into the shadow registers (RTC_SSR, RTC_TR and RTC_DR). This bit is cleared by hardware in initialization mode, while a shift operation is pending (SHPF = 1), or when in bypass shadow register mode (BYPSHAD = 1). This bit can also be cleared by software. It is cleared either by software or by hardware in initialization mode.

enum_values.name: NotSynced
enum_values.description: Calendar shadow registers not yet synchronized

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: This flag is cleared by software by writing 0

fields.name: INITF
fields.description: Initialization flag When this bit is set to 1, the RTC is in initialization state, and the time, date and prescaler registers can be updated.

enum_values.name: NotAllowed
enum_values.description: Calendar registers update is not allowed

fields.name: INIT
fields.description: Initialization mode

enum_values.name: InitMode
enum_values.description: Initialization mode used to program time and date register (RTC_TR and RTC_DR), and prescaler register (RTC_PRER). Counters are stopped and start counting from the new value when INIT is reset.

fields.name: BIN
fields.description: Binary mode

fields.name: BCDU
fields.description: BCD update (BIN = 10 or 11) In mixed mode when both BCD calendar and binary extended counter are used (BIN = 10 or 11), the calendar second is incremented using the SSR Least Significant Bits.

fields.name: RECALPF
fields.description: Recalibration pending Flag The RECALPF status flag is automatically set to 1 when software writes to the RTC_CALR register, indicating that the RTC_CALR register is blocked. When the new calibration settings are taken into account, this bit returns to 0. Refer to .

enum_values.name: Pending
enum_values.description: The RECALPF status flag is automatically set to 1 when software writes to the RTC_CALR register, indicating that the RTC_CALR register is blocked. When the new calibration settings are taken into account, this bit returns to 0

fields.name: PREDIV_S
fields.description: Synchronous prescaler factor This is the synchronous division factor: ck_spre frequency = ck_apre frequency/(PREDIV_S+1)

fields.name: PREDIV_A
fields.description: Asynchronous prescaler factor This is the asynchronous division factor: ck_apre frequency = RTCCLK frequency/(PREDIV_A+1)

fields.name: WUT
fields.description: Wakeup auto-reload value bits When the wakeup timer is enabled (WUTE set to 1), the WUTF flag is set every (WUT[15:0] + 1) ck_wut cycles. The ck_wut period is selected through WUCKSEL[2:0] bits of the RTC_CR register. When WUCKSEL[2] = 1, the wakeup timer becomes 17-bits and WUCKSEL[1] effectively becomes WUT[16] the most-significant bit to be reloaded into the timer. The first assertion of WUTF occurs between WUT and (WUT + 2) ck_wut cycles after WUTE is set. Setting WUT[15:0] to 0x0000 with WUCKSEL[2:0] = 011 (RTCCLK/2) is forbidden.

fields.name: WUTOCLR
fields.description: Wakeup auto-reload output clear value When WUTOCLR[15:0] is different from 0x0000, WUTF is set by hardware when the auto-reload down-counter reaches 0 and is cleared by hardware when the auto-reload downcounter reaches WUTOCLR[15:0]. When WUTOCLR[15:0] = 0x0000, WUTF is set by hardware when the WUT down-counter reaches 0 and is cleared by software.

fields.name: WUCKSEL
fields.description: ck_wut wakeup clock selection 10x: ck_spre (usually 1 Hz) clock is selected in BCD mode. In binary or mixed mode, this is the clock selected by BCDU. 11x: ck_spre (usually 1 Hz) clock is selected in BCD mode. In binary or mixed mode, this is the clock selected by BCDU. Furthermore, 216 is added to the WUT counter value.

enum_values.name: ClockSpareWithOffset
enum_values.description: ck_spre (usually 1 Hz) clock is selected and 2^16 is added to the WUT counter value

fields.name: TSEDGE
fields.description: Timestamp event active edge TSE must be reset when TSEDGE is changed to avoid unwanted TSF setting.

enum_values.name: RisingEdge
enum_values.description: RTC_TS input rising edge generates a time-stamp event

enum_values.name: FallingEdge
enum_values.description: RTC_TS input falling edge generates a time-stamp event

fields.name: REFCKON
fields.description: RTC_REFIN reference clock detection enable (50 or 60 Hz) Note: BIN must be 0x00 and PREDIV_S must be 0x00FF.

fields.name: BYPSHAD
fields.description: Bypass the shadow registers Note: If the frequency of the APB1 clock is less than seven times the frequency of RTCCLK, BYPSHAD must be set to 1.

enum_values.name: ShadowReg
enum_values.description: Calendar values (when reading from RTC_SSR, RTC_TR, and RTC_DR) are taken from the shadow registers, which are updated once every two RTCCLK cycles

enum_values.name: BypassShadowReg
enum_values.description: Calendar values (when reading from RTC_SSR, RTC_TR, and RTC_DR) are taken directly from the calendar counters

fields.name: FMT
fields.description: Hour format

fields.name: SSRUIE
fields.description: SSR underflow interrupt enable

fields.name: ALR%sE
fields.description: Alarm %s enable

fields.name: WUTE
fields.description: Wakeup timer enable Note: When the wakeup timer is disabled, wait for WUTWF = 1 before enabling it again.

fields.name: TSE
fields.description: timestamp enable

fields.name: ALR%sIE
fields.description: Alarm %s interrupt enable

fields.name: WUTIE
fields.description: Wakeup timer interrupt enable

fields.name: TSIE
fields.description: Timestamp interrupt enable

fields.name: ADD1H
fields.description: Add 1 hour (summer time change) When this bit is set outside initialization mode, 1 hour is added to the calendar time. This bit is always read as 0.

enum_values.name: Add1
enum_values.description: Adds 1 hour to the current time. This can be used for summer time change outside initialization mode

fields.name: SUB1H
fields.description: Subtract 1 hour (winter time change) When this bit is set outside initialization mode, 1 hour is subtracted to the calendar time if the current hour is not 0. This bit is always read as 0. Setting this bit has no effect when current hour is 0.

enum_values.name: Sub1
enum_values.description: Subtracts 1 hour to the current time. This can be used for winter time change outside initialization mode

fields.name: BKP
fields.description: Backup This bit can be written by the user to memorize whether the daylight saving time change has been performed or not.

enum_values.name: DSTNotChanged
enum_values.description: Daylight Saving Time change has not been performed

enum_values.name: DSTChanged
enum_values.description: Daylight Saving Time change has been performed

fields.name: COSEL
fields.description: Calibration output selection When COE = 1, this bit selects which signal is output on CALIB. These frequencies are valid for RTCCLK at 32.768 kHz and prescalers at their default values (PREDIV_A = 127 and PREDIV_S = 255). Refer to .

enum_values.name: CalFreq_512Hz
enum_values.description: Calibration output is 512 Hz (with default prescaler setting)

enum_values.name: CalFreq_1Hz
enum_values.description: Calibration output is 1 Hz (with default prescaler setting)

fields.name: POL
fields.description: Output polarity This bit is used to configure the polarity of TAMPALRM output.

enum_values.name: High
enum_values.description: The pin is high when ALRAF/ALRBF/WUTF is asserted (depending on OSEL[1:0])

enum_values.name: Low
enum_values.description: The pin is low when ALRAF/ALRBF/WUTF is asserted (depending on OSEL[1:0])

fields.name: OSEL
fields.description: Output selection These bits are used to select the flag to be routed to TAMPALRM output.

fields.name: COE
fields.description: Calibration output enable This bit enables the CALIB output

fields.name: ITSE
fields.description: timestamp on internal event enable

fields.name: TAMPTS
fields.description: Activate timestamp on tamper detection event TAMPTS is valid even if TSE = 0 in the RTC_CR register. Timestamp flag is set up to 3 ck_apre cycles after the tamper flags.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Tamper detection event does not cause a RTC timestamp to be saved

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Save RTC timestamp on tamper detection event

fields.name: TAMPOE
fields.description: Tamper detection output enable on TAMPALRM

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: The tamper flag is not routed on TAMPALRM

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: The tamper flag is routed on TAMPALRM, combined with the signal provided by OSEL and with the polarity provided by POL

fields.name: ALRAFCLR
fields.description: Alarm A flag automatic clear

fields.name: ALRBFCLR
fields.description: Alarm B flag automatic clear

fields.name: TAMPALRM_PU
fields.description: TAMPALRM pull-up enable

enum_values.name: NoPullUp
enum_values.description: No pull-up is applied on TAMPALRM output

enum_values.name: PullUp
enum_values.description: A pull-up is applied on TAMPALRM output

fields.name: TAMPALRM_TYPE
fields.description: TAMPALRM output type

fields.name: OUT2EN
fields.description: RTC_OUT2 output enable Setting this bit permits to remap the RTC outputs on RTC_OUT2 as follows: OUT2EN = 0: RTC output 2 disable If OSEL different 00 or TAMPOE = 1: TAMPALRM is output on RTC_OUT1 If OSEL = 00 and TAMPOE = 0 and COE = 1: CALIB is output on RTC_OUT1 OUT2EN = 1: RTC output 2 enable If (OSEL different 00 or TAMPOE = 1) and COE = 0: TAMPALRM is output on RTC_OUT2 If OSEL = 00 and TAMPOE = 0 and COE = 1: CALIB is output on RTC_OUT2 If (OSEL different 00 or TAMPOE = 1) and COE = 1: CALIB is output on RTC_OUT2 and TAMPALRM is output on RTC_OUT1.

fields.name: ALRAPRIV
fields.description: Alarm A and SSR underflow privilege protection

fields.name: ALRBPRIV
fields.description: Alarm B privilege protection

fields.name: WUTPRIV
fields.description: Wakeup timer privilege protection

fields.name: TSPRIV
fields.description: Timestamp privilege protection

fields.name: CALPRIV
fields.description: Shift register, Delight saving, calibration and reference clock privilege protection

fields.name: INITPRIV
fields.description: Initialization privilege protection

fields.name: PRIV
fields.description: RTC privilege protection

fields.name: ALRASEC
fields.description: Alarm A and SSR underflow protection

fields.name: ALRBSEC
fields.description: Alarm B protection

fields.name: WUTSEC
fields.description: Wakeup timer protection

fields.name: TSSEC
fields.description: Timestamp protection

fields.name: CALSEC
fields.description: Shift register, daylight saving, calibration and reference clock protection

fields.name: INITSEC
fields.description: Initialization protection

fields.name: SEC
fields.description: RTC global protection

fields.name: KEY
fields.description: Write protection key This byte is written by software. Reading this byte always returns 0x00. Refer to for a description of how to unlock RTC register write protection.

enum_values.name: Activate
enum_values.description: Activate write protection (any value that is not the keys)

fields.name: CALM
fields.description: Calibration minus The frequency of the calendar is reduced by masking CALM out of 220 RTCCLK pulses (32 seconds if the input frequency is 32768 Hz). This decreases the frequency of the calendar with a resolution of 0.9537 ppm. To increase the frequency of the calendar, this feature should be used in conjunction with CALP. See .

fields.name: LPCAL
fields.description: RTC low-power mode

fields.name: CALW16
fields.description: Use a 16-second calibration cycle period When CALW16 is set to 1, the 16-second calibration cycle period is selected. This bit must not be set to 1 if CALW8 = 1. Note: CALM[0] is stuck at 0 when CALW16 = 1. Refer to calibration.

enum_values.name: SixteenSeconds
enum_values.description: When CALW16 is set to ‘1’, the 16-second calibration cycle period is selected.This bit must not be set to ‘1’ if CALW8=1

fields.name: CALW8
fields.description: Use an 8-second calibration cycle period When CALW8 is set to 1, the 8-second calibration cycle period is selected. Note: CALM[1:0] are stuck at 00 when CALW8 = 1. Refer to digital calibration.

enum_values.name: EightSeconds
enum_values.description: When CALW8 is set to ‘1’, the 8-second calibration cycle period is selected

fields.name: CALP
fields.description: Increase frequency of RTC by 488.5 ppm This feature is intended to be used in conjunction with CALM, which lowers the frequency of the calendar with a fine resolution. if the input frequency is 32768 Hz, the number of RTCCLK pulses added during a 32-second window is calculated as follows: (512 CALP) CALM. Refer to .

enum_values.name: IncreaseFreq
enum_values.description: One RTCCLK pulse is effectively inserted every 2^11 pulses (frequency increased by 488.5 ppm)

fields.name: SUBFS
fields.description: Subtract a fraction of a second These bits are write only and is always read as zero. Writing to this bit has no effect when a shift operation is pending (when SHPF = 1, in RTC_ICSR). The value which is written to SUBFS is added to the synchronous prescaler counter. Since this counter counts down, this operation effectively subtracts from (delays) the clock by: Delay (seconds) = SUBFS / (PREDIV_S + 1) A fraction of a second can effectively be added to the clock (advancing the clock) when the ADD1S function is used in conjunction with SUBFS, effectively advancing the clock by: Advance (seconds) = (1 (SUBFS / (PREDIV_S + 1))). In mixed BCD-binary mode (BIN=10 or 11), the SUBFS[14:BCDU+8] must be written with 0. Note: Writing to SUBFS causes RSF to be cleared. Software can then wait until RSF = 1 to be sure that the shadow registers have been updated with the shifted time.

fields.name: ADD1S
fields.description: Add one second This bit is write only and is always read as zero. Writing to this bit has no effect when a shift operation is pending (when SHPF = 1, in RTC_ICSR). This function is intended to be used with SUBFS (see description below) in order to effectively add a fraction of a second to the clock in an atomic operation.

enum_values.name: Add1
enum_values.description: Add one second to the clock/calendar

fields.name: SU
fields.description: Second units in BCD format

fields.name: ST
fields.description: Second tens in BCD format

fields.name: MSK1
fields.description: Alarm seconds mask

enum_values.name: Mask
enum_values.description: Alarm set if the date/day match

enum_values.name: NotMask
enum_values.description: Date/day don’t care in Alarm comparison

fields.name: MNU
fields.description: Minute units in BCD format

fields.name: MNT
fields.description: Minute tens in BCD format

fields.name: MSK2
fields.description: Alarm minutes mask

fields.name: HU
fields.description: Hour units in BCD format

fields.name: HT
fields.description: Hour tens in BCD format

fields.name: PM
fields.description: AM/PM notation

fields.name: MSK3
fields.description: Alarm hours mask

fields.name: DU
fields.description: Date units or day in BCD format

fields.name: DT
fields.description: Date tens in BCD format

fields.name: WDSEL
fields.description: Week day selection

enum_values.name: WeekDay
enum_values.description: DU[3:0] represents the week day. DT[1:0] is don’t care.

fields.name: MSK4
fields.description: Alarm date mask

fields.name: SS
fields.description: Sub seconds value This value is compared with the contents of the synchronous prescaler counter to determine if alarm A is to be activated. Only bits 0 up MASKSS-1 are compared. This field is the mirror of SS[14:0] in the RTC_ALRMABINR, and so can also be read or written through RTC_ALRMABINR.

fields.name: MASKSS
fields.description: Mask the most-significant bits starting at this bit 2: SS[31:2] are don't care in Alarm A comparison. Only SS[1:0] are compared. ... 31: SS[31] is don't care in Alarm A comparison. Only SS[30:0] are compared. From 32 to 63: All 32 SS bits are compared and must match to activate alarm. Note: In BCD mode (BIN=00) the overflow bits of the synchronous counter (bits 31:15) are never compared. These bits can be different from 0 only after a shift operation.

fields.name: SSCLR
fields.description: Clear synchronous counter on alarm (Binary mode only) Note: SSCLR must be kept to 0 when BCD or mixed mode is used (BIN = 00, 10 or 11).

fields.name: ALR%sF
fields.description: Alarm %s flag

enum_values.name: Match
enum_values.description: This flag is set by hardware when the time/date registers (RTC_TR and RTC_DR) match the Alarm register (RTC_ALRxBR)

fields.name: WUTF
fields.description: Wakeup timer flag This flag is set by hardware when the wakeup auto-reload counter reaches 0. If WUTOCLR[15:0] is different from 0x0000, WUTF is cleared by hardware when the wakeup auto-reload counter reaches WUTOCLR value. If WUTOCLR[15:0] is 0x0000, WUTF must be cleared by software. This flag must be cleared by software at least 1.5 RTCCLK periods before WUTF is set to 1 again.

enum_values.name: Zero
enum_values.description: This flag is set by hardware when the wakeup auto-reload counter reaches 0

fields.name: TSF
fields.description: Timestamp flag This flag is set by hardware when a timestamp event occurs. If ITSF flag is set, TSF must be cleared together with ITSF. Note: TSF is not set if TAMPTS = 1 and the tamper flag is read during the 3 ck_apre cycles following tamper event. Refer to for more details.

enum_values.name: TimestampEvent
enum_values.description: This flag is set by hardware when a time-stamp event occurs

fields.name: TSOVF
fields.description: Timestamp overflow flag This flag is set by hardware when a timestamp event occurs while TSF is already set. It is recommended to check and then clear TSOVF only after clearing the TSF bit. Otherwise, an overflow might not be noticed if a timestamp event occurs immediately before the TSF bit is cleared.

enum_values.name: Overflow
enum_values.description: This flag is set by hardware when a time-stamp event occurs while TSF is already set

fields.name: ITSF
fields.description: Internal timestamp flag This flag is set by hardware when a timestamp on the internal event occurs.

enum_values.name: TimestampEvent
enum_values.description: This flag is set by hardware when a timestamp on the internal event occurs

fields.name: SSRUF
fields.description: SSR underflow flag This flag is set by hardware when the SSR rolls under 0. SSRUF is not set when SSCLR=1.

registers.name: MISR
registers.description: RTC non-secure masked interrupt status register

fields.name: ALR%sMF
fields.description: Alarm %s masked flag

enum_values.name: Match
enum_values.description: This flag is set by hardware when the time/date registers (RTC_TR and RTC_DR) match the Alarm register (RTC_ALRMxR)

fields.name: WUTMF
fields.description: Wakeup timer non-secure masked flag This flag is set by hardware when the wakeup timer non-secure interrupt occurs. This flag must be cleared by software at least 1.5 RTCCLK periods before WUTF is set to 1 again.

enum_values.name: Zero
enum_values.description: This flag is set by hardware when the wakeup auto-reload counter reaches 0

fields.name: TSMF
fields.description: Timestamp non-secure masked flag This flag is set by hardware when a timestamp non-secure interrupt occurs. If ITSF flag is set, TSF must be cleared together with ITSF.

enum_values.name: TimestampEvent
enum_values.description: This flag is set by hardware when a time-stamp event occurs

fields.name: TSOVMF
fields.description: Timestamp overflow non-secure masked flag This flag is set by hardware when a timestamp interrupt occurs while TSMF is already set. It is recommended to check and then clear TSOVF only after clearing the TSF bit. Otherwise, an overflow might not be noticed if a timestamp event occurs immediately before the TSF bit is cleared.

enum_values.name: Overflow
enum_values.description: This flag is set by hardware when a time-stamp event occurs while TSF is already set

fields.name: ITSMF
fields.description: Internal timestamp non-secure masked flag This flag is set by hardware when a timestamp on the internal event occurs and timestamp non-secure interrupt is raised.

enum_values.name: TimestampEvent
enum_values.description: This flag is set by hardware when a timestamp on the internal event occurs

fields.name: SSRUMF
fields.description: SSR underflow non-secure masked flag This flag is set by hardware when the SSR underflow non-secure interrupt occurs.

registers.name: SMISR
registers.description: RTC secure masked interrupt status register

fields.name: ALRAMF
fields.description: Alarm A interrupt secure masked flag This flag is set by hardware when the alarm A secure interrupt occurs.

fields.name: ALRBMF
fields.description: Alarm B interrupt secure masked flag This flag is set by hardware when the alarm B secure interrupt occurs.

fields.name: WUTMF
fields.description: Wakeup timer interrupt secure masked flag This flag is set by hardware when the wakeup timer secure interrupt occurs. This flag must be cleared by software at least 1.5 RTCCLK periods before WUTF is set to 1 again.

fields.name: TSMF
fields.description: Timestamp interrupt secure masked flag This flag is set by hardware when a timestamp secure interrupt occurs. If ITSF flag is set, TSF must be cleared together with ITSF.

fields.name: TSOVMF
fields.description: Timestamp overflow interrupt secure masked flag This flag is set by hardware when a timestamp secure interrupt occurs while TSMF is already set. It is recommended to check and then clear TSOVF only after clearing the TSF bit. Otherwise, an overflow might not be noticed if a timestamp event occurs immediately before the TSF bit is cleared.

fields.name: ITSMF
fields.description: Internal timestamp interrupt secure masked flag This flag is set by hardware when a timestamp on the internal event occurs and timestamp secure interrupt is raised.

fields.name: SSRUMF
fields.description: SSR underflow secure masked flag This flag is set by hardware when the SSR underflow secure interrupt occurs.

fields.name: CALRAF
fields.description: Clear alarm A flag Writing 1 in this bit clears the ALRAF bit in the RTC_SR register.

fields.name: CALRBF
fields.description: Clear alarm B flag Writing 1 in this bit clears the ALRBF bit in the RTC_SR register.

fields.name: CWUTF
fields.description: Clear wakeup timer flag Writing 1 in this bit clears the WUTF bit in the RTC_SR register.

fields.name: CTSF
fields.description: Clear timestamp flag Writing 1 in this bit clears the TSOVF bit in the RTC_SR register. If ITSF flag is set, TSF must be cleared together with ITSF by setting CRSF and CITSF.

fields.name: CTSOVF
fields.description: Clear timestamp overflow flag Writing 1 in this bit clears the TSOVF bit in the RTC_SR register. It is recommended to check and then clear TSOVF only after clearing the TSF bit. Otherwise, an overflow might not be noticed if a timestamp event occurs immediately before the TSF bit is cleared.

fields.name: CITSF
fields.description: Clear internal timestamp flag Writing 1 in this bit clears the ITSF bit in the RTC_SR register.

fields.name: CSSRUF
fields.description: Clear SSR underflow flag Writing '1' in this bit clears the SSRUF in the RTC_SR register.

fields.name: OUT2_RMP
fields.description: RTC_OUT2 mapping

fields.name: SS
fields.description: Synchronous counter alarm value in Binary mode This value is compared with the contents of the synchronous counter to determine if Alarm A is to be activated. Only bits 0 up MASKSS-1 are compared. SS[14:0] is the mirror of SS[14:0] in the RTC_ALRMASSRR, and so can also be read or written through RTC_ALRMASSR.
